product_overview: IES is an AI-powered recruitment platform that connects candidates and recruiters — enabling account creation, profile and resume management, job discovery and applications, and recruiter job management. This PRD covers the primary, free-access frontend flows for onboarding and basic account operations.
core_goals: Allow users to discover the product and start the signup or login process from the public landing page.
Enable users to create accounts (candidate or recruiter/company) and be automatically logged in after registration.
Allow existing users to authenticate with email and password to access their dashboards.
Provide a secure password-recovery flow that preserves privacy (no disclosure whether an email exists).

features.name: Landing / Product Discovery
features.description: Public landing experience where visitors learn about IES, choose to register or sign in, and are guided to the appropriate onboarding path.
user_flows: Navigate to /landing -> Read product overview and available user roles -> Navigate to /register -> Choose account type to begin registration
Navigate to /landing -> Navigate to /login -> See login form and option to recover password

features.name: User Login
features.description: Authenticate existing users (candidates or recruiters) with email and password so they can access their dashboard and protected features.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click Login button to submit -> See candidate or recruiter dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Fill email input field with invalid email format -> Fill password input field -> Click Login button to submit -> See client-side validation error about email format

features.name: User Registration (Candidate)
features.description: Create a candidate account by providing personal details and a strong password; successful registration returns a token and auto-logs the user in.
user_flows: Navigate to /register -> Select candidate account type -> Fill first name -> Fill last name -> Fill email -> Fill password meeting strength requirements -> Submit registration form -> See candidate dashboard (auto-logged in)
Navigate to /register -> Select candidate account type -> Fill fields but enter a weak password -> Submit registration form -> See client-side validation error instructing stronger password

features.name: User Registration (Company / Recruiter Creating Company)
features.description: Create a recruiter account while registering a new company; the registering recruiter becomes the company's Admin and is logged in after registration.
user_flows: Navigate to /register -> Select company/ recruiter account type -> Fill admin first name -> Fill admin last name -> Fill admin email -> Fill admin password meeting strength requirements -> Fill company name -> Fill company tax number -> Submit registration form -> See recruiter/company dashboard (auto-logged in)

features.name: User Registration (Recruiter via Invite Code)
features.description: Allow a recruiter to join an existing company using an invite code during registration and be auto-logged in after successful registration.
user_flows: Navigate to /register -> Select recruiter (join via invite) account type -> Fill first name -> Fill last name -> Fill email -> Fill password meeting strength requirements -> Enter invite code -> Submit registration form -> See recruiter dashboard (auto-logged in)

Navigate to /register -> Select recruiter (join via invite) account type -> Fill fields but leave invite code blank -> Submit registration form -> See client-side validation error asking for invite code

features.name: Password Recovery (Forgot Password)
features.description: Allow users who forgot their password to request a password reset in a privacy-preserving way; the UI shows a generic confirmation regardless of whether the email exists.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Choose forgot password flow -> Fill email address -> Submit forgot password request -> See confirmation message: 'If this email exists, a reset link has been sent.'
Navigate to /login -> Choose forgot password flow -> Fill malformed email address -> Submit forgot password request -> See client-side validation error about email format

features.name: User Login
features.description: Login with email and password credentials
